topic: κ Coupling Cluster — three-citation verification: Kim & Chang DOI .024→.022 (ISS-1999), Bernard→Hinderer 2022 J9 paper (ISS-2000), Monte Carlo softening (ISS-1988)
change_summary: Three narrow self-apply patches landed in SEC-6.14 in decider run 2026-05-12-17-20: (1) Kim & Chang 2018 DOI '10.1016/j.asr.2018.01.024' (Sadeghi & Emami) corrected to '.022' (real Kim & Chang Adv. Space Res. paper) in 'What κ predicts during past eclipses' subsection (ISS-1999, narrow gate); (2) 'Bernard et al. 2022, Pure Appl. Geophys. 179, 1701-1727' (wrong first author per Crossref) corrected to 'Hinderer et al. 2022' in 'What past SG data actually shows' subsection (ISS-2000, narrow gate); (3) Monte Carlo r>0.96 assertion in 'The storm corollary (WIN-058b)' subsection softened from 'produces r > 0.96 from shared forcing alone, as the WIN-058b detail already documents' to 'our Monte Carlo simulation (documented in WIN-058b detail) finds r > 0.96 from shared forcing alone' — removing the implicit methodological-citation burden (ISS-1988, BAU narrow patch).
our_argument_summary: SEC-6.14 retains its structural argument: dome publishes κ = 1.677 nT/µGal (WIN-012), which applied to documented 1999 European and 2009 East Asian eclipse-magnetic perturbations (5-15 nT range) predicts contact-phase gravity pulses of 3-9 µGal at Membach (1999) and Chinese SG sites (2009) — both campaigns recorded zero, refuting the dome's own coupling. The two-horned vise (either κ couples generally and 1999/2009 refute it, or κ has unpublished scope conditions that also destroy PRED-TIER-3's derivation) is unchanged by these patches. The patches only fix citation hygiene and tighten methodological framing — they do not alter the empirical refutation chain.

relevant_files: data/sections.json:part6.html (SEC-6.14 subsections 'What κ predicts during past eclipses', 'What past SG data actually shows', 'The storm corollary (WIN-058b)')
data/wins.json:058b (WIN-058b Monte Carlo cross-reference target — verified to actually document the Monte Carlo at SNR=10, τ=30s, N=1620 samples, mean r=0.990, 90% CI 0.985-0.994)
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: No monitor.py code paths. All three patches are citation/prose substitutions; no derivations or numerical claims changed.
data_sources_used: Kim & Chang 2018 Adv. Space Res. 61(8) 2040-2049 DOI 10.1016/j.asr.2018.01.022 — confirmed via PII S0273117718300656 / ADS bibcode 2018AdSpR..61.2040K

Hinderer et al. 2022, Pure Appl. Geophys. 179, 1701-1727, DOI 10.1007/s00024-022-03000-4 — confirmed via Springer Link; full title 'Intercomparing Superconducting Gravimeter Records in a Dense Meter-Scale Network at the J9 Gravimetric Observatory of Strasbourg, France', first author Hinderer
Crossley, Hinderer & Riccardi 2013 Rep. Prog. Phys. 76 046101 DOI 10.1088/0034-4885/76/4/046101 — unchanged across patches, still correctly cited
WIN-058b Monte Carlo simulation (internal cross-reference) — confirmed actually documented at data/wins.json:058b:detail_evidence with explicit parameters and result
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: Unchanged. The vise structure ('What the dome could do' subsection) still forecloses every published escape route via independently-timestamped scope conditions.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None. All three patches are surgical text fixes; no claims about dome code were touched.

kernel_of_truth.description: Verification scope only. The Hinderer et al. 2022 J9 paper (data/sections.json:part6.html SEC-6.14 'What past SG data actually shows') is a genuine state-of-the-art metrological intercomparison of eight SGs in a dense meter-scale network — a legitimate reference for modern SG residual noise floors at 0.1-1 µGal. Similarly Kim & Chang 2018 (DOI 10.1016/j.asr.2018.01.022) is a real eclipse-magnetic ensemble study (39 eclipses, 207 measurements, 100 INTERMAGNET observatories) the dome could legitimately cite for ΔB context. The dome could in principle build a κ argument on top of these real datasets.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The kernel — that eclipse magnetic perturbations and modern SG noise floors are independently characterized — actually strengthens our refutation, not the dome's claim: if Kim & Chang 2018 (data/sections.json: SEC-6.14) confirms eclipse ΔB ~5-15 nT for high-coverage events, and Hinderer et al. 2022 confirms modern SG residual noise floors ~0.1-1 µGal, then the predicted few-µGal κ-pulse should be 30-600× above noise — exactly the gap our vise depends on. The dome's escape requires denying numbers from papers it would otherwise cite.

verification_checks.check: Monte Carlo r>0.96 prose softening landed in SEC-6.14
verification_checks.evidence: Old phrasing 'Monte Carlo at physically realistic SNR produces r > 0.96 from shared forcing alone, as the WIN-058b detail already documents' is gone (grep -c 'WIN-058b detail already documents' data/sections.json returns 0). New phrasing 'our Monte Carlo simulation (documented in WIN-058b detail) finds r > 0.96 from shared forcing alone' is present (grep -c 'finds r > 0.96 from shared forcing alone' data/sections.json returns 1) in 'The storm corollary (WIN-058b)' subsection. Removes the implicit peer-reviewed-citation burden the c3 review flagged.

verification_checks.check: Cross-reference integrity: WIN-058b actually documents the Monte Carlo claim
verification_checks.evidence: data/wins.json:058b:detail_evidence explicitly documents the Monte Carlo with parameters: 'Monte Carlo simulation of two independent sensors responding to a shared storm-envelope driver with autocorrelated noise (τ = 30 s, N = 1,620 samples...) ... At physically realistic SNR = 10 ... the simulation produces mean r = 0.990 (90% CI: 0.985-0.994)'. The 'r > 0.96 from shared forcing alone' claim in SEC-6.14 is actually conservative against WIN-058b's documented r > 0.985 lower-bound — strengthening the cross-reference rather than weakening it.

verification_checks.check: Surrounding prose intact (no collateral edits, structural argument unchanged)
verification_checks.evidence: Section length 111502 chars (part6.html). The vise mechanism in 'What the dome could do' subsection, the storm corollary, the cross-reference to WIN registry, and the 'How κ was derived' subsection all remain unchanged. The Crossley et al. 2013 separate citation (DOI 10.1088/0034-4885/76/4/046101) is preserved and correctly linked — by splitting it from the formerly-mismatched J9 citation, the c1/c3 H5 URL/year mismatch concern is also addressed (the 2022 J9 paper now has its own attribution to Hinderer, separate from the 2013 Crossley RoPP review).

text_fingerprint.subsection_focus: What κ predicts during past eclipses + What past SG data actually shows + The storm corollary (WIN-058b)

deeper_analysis: All three EXP-358-introduced or carried-forward citation/prose bugs that triggered ISS-1988, ISS-1999, ISS-2000 are now fixed in data/sections.json (part6.html SEC-6.14) and propagated to docs/index.html. The patches are surgical and do not touch the vise mechanism or the empirical refutation chain. Notably the Bernard→Hinderer fix also resolves part of the SEC-6.14.c1 H5 concern about URL/year mismatch between Crossley 2013 and the 2022 PAG paper — by splitting them into separate citations (each with correct attribution), the J9-intercomparison reference is now coherent. Out-of-scope: the c3 review's 15 holes include numerous CARRY-FORWARDs (ISS-1964 through ISS-1977) and 5 NEW-IN-c3 items that the current patches do not address. None of these are introduced or worsened by the current patches. The decider should track these separately under their existing ISSs — they remain open. No new holes detected during this verification.

summary_for_decider: Queue_id 340 (SEC-6.14 three-citation verification): PASS. All three narrow patches landed cleanly in data/sections.json and propagated to docs/index.html: (1) Kim & Chang DOI .022 — web-verified to real paper; (2) Bernard→Hinderer 2022 J9 paper — web-verified Hinderer is first author of DOI 10.1007/s00024-022-03000-4; (3) Monte Carlo softening to 'our Monte Carlo simulation (documented in WIN-058b detail) finds r > 0.96' — cross-reference target WIN-058b confirmed to actually document the MC with explicit parameters and mean r=0.990 (90% CI 0.985-0.994), which is conservative against the 0.96 floor. Recommend close ISS-1988, ISS-1999, ISS-2000. The c3 review's 15 open holes (CARRY-FORWARDs ISS-1964/65/66/72/73/74/75/76/77, plus 5 new-in-c3 items) remain open and unaddressed by these patches — track separately. Batch-position 2 of 2 (final of this curmudgeon run; PRED-106 deferred — see batch report).
